os: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4.z for Power, Little Endian
scenario: Spinlock Deadlock with Soft Lockups and RCU Stalls
symptoms: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#19 stuck for 23s! [migration/19:127]
rcu: INFO: rcu_sched detected stalls on CPUs/tasks:
CPU 8 stuck waiting on rq.lock spinlock of CPU 63 that is not locked

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for soft lockup and RCU stall messages
workflow.action: log | grep -e rcu_sched\ detected -e soft\ lockup
workflow.observation: Multiple soft lockup messages and RCU stall warnings

workflow.thought: Examine multi_stop_data structure for CPU 19
workflow.observation: active_cpus field pointing to cpumask bitmap

workflow.thought: Check cpumask to see which CPU is active
workflow.observation: bits field shows CPU 8 is active

workflow.thought: Evaluate binary value to confirm CPU 8
Check call_single_queue for pending IPIs
workflow.observation: Only CPU 8 has pending items in call_single_queue

workflow.thought: Examine call_single_data_t list for functions
Check multi_stop_data for CPU 40
workflow.observation: active_cpus field pointing to another cpumask

workflow.thought: Examine cpumask for CPU 40's active CPUs
workflow.observation: bits field shows CPU 48 is active

workflow.thought: Evaluate binary to confirm CPU 48
Get backtrace of CPU 8 to see what it's doing
Check process state for task on CPU 8
Disassemble update_blocked_averages to see spinlock call
Find which runqueue CPU 8 is waiting on
Get address of rq.lock spinlock
Check if spinlock is actually locked
Disassemble __spin_yield to see hypervisor call
Disassemble plpar_hcall_norets to see current location
workflow.observation: At lwz instruction after hypervisor call

workflow.thought: Check timer queue for CPU 8
workflow.observation: Many overdue timers in hrtimer queue

workflow.thought: Check sysctl settings for panic behavior
Check softlockup panic setting

root_cause_analysis: The CPU is not processing timer interrupts properly due to some hardware glitch.
